en.name: Lactate in arterial blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Lactate is the end-product of anaerobic glucose metabolism; its concentration in arterial blood is a key marker of tissue hypoperfusion and is widely used in shock, sepsis, and critical care [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser or a point-of-care device on arterial blood. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. **Note: arterial vs venous lactate.** Lactate concentrations in arterial and venous blood are **not numerically equivalent** — the arterio-venous difference reflects tissue lactate production/clearance and is clinically meaningful. This concept set covers **arterial lactate** only. Venous lactate (including capillary, mixed venous, central venous, serum/plasma generic) is captured by a separate concept set, "Lactate in venous blood". Cord blood and fetus specimens are excluded. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on two LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. The set resolves to three LOINC "Lab Test" concepts. ### Arterial blood - *3018405 / 2518-9 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code for arterial lactate. - *3035561 / 30242-2 — Lactate [Mass/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3020410 / 32132-3 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Arterial plasma (LOINC)*: less common variant where the specimen is explicitly arterial plasma rather than whole arterial blood. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.111 mmol/L` (molar mass of lactate = 89.07 g/mol). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Venous, mixed venous, capillary, central venous, generic Blood, Serum or Plasma specimens**: captured by the separate concept set "Lactate in venous blood". - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **Lactate/Pyruvate ratio**: different measurand. - **Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH)**: different analyte, captured by the separate "Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) in blood" concept set. - **CSF, body fluid, dialysis fluid and other specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a lactate value explicitly from arterial blood (e.g. blood gas analyser, arterial sample): `3018405 / 2518-9 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood`. - **Source reports a lactate value without specifying arterial origin**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use the separate "Lactate in venous blood" concept set, which aggregates venous, capillary, mixed venous and generic specimens. - **Do not confuse with**: venous lactate (separate concept set, arterio-venous difference is clinically meaningful), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH, different analyte), lactate/pyruvate ratio. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2518-9 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2518-9/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
